title: BL-17
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Droid |type=Mandalorian |image=[[File:BL17-ARTTF.png]] |name=BL-17 |homeworld= |birth=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]The [[BL-series Battle Legionnaire]]s were created after the founding of the [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. |death=[[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]]"[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]" in [[Polyhedron 170|''Polyhedron'' 170]] states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before {{Film|IV}}. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of '' A New Hope'' in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], meaning that ''Star Wars: Droids'' takes place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]. In {{DroidsCite|A Race to the Finish}}, BL-17 is shown destroyed. |creator=[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |manufacturer=[[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]] |line= |model=[[BL-series Battle Legionnaire]] |class=[[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droid]] |cost= |length= |width= |height=1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|serieslink=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} |sensor=Orange{{DroidsCite|A Race to the Finish}} |plating= |armament=[[Briletto Company|Briletto]] [[AAP-II blaster box|AAP-II "blaster box"]] |equipment=*First aid kit (internal) *Signal jammer *Balance gyro |affiliation=*[[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] *[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] }} {{Quote|If it isn't BL-17, the lowest form of synthetic life to ever crawl across a planet.|Proto One|}} '''BL-17''' was a [[BL-series Battle Legionnaire]], created to serve the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. One of the few Battle Legionnaires to survive the [[Mission to Norval II]], which saw the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] defeat the Confederate-allied [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]], BL-17 later came into the possession of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] sometime after the [[Declaration of a New Order]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. When Fett was approached by [[Sise Fromm]] to hunt the [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeder]] [[Joben-Dusat Racing Team|racing team]] of [[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]], [[Jord Dusat]], and [[Kea Moll]] on [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]], the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter decided to use BL-17 to infiltrate the group by befriending their [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]. BL-17 succeeded in leading the group into a trap, but before Fett could attack, his true intentions were revealed in a chance encounter with a former acquaintance, the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Proto One]]. In the ensuing skirmish, BL-17 was knocked into a pile of scrap machinery, causing it to fall and crush him. ==Biography== ===Clone Wars=== [[File:BL17d.jpg|thumb|left|200px|BL-17 and Boba Fett]] BL-17 was a [[BL-series Battle Legionnaire]], one of only 1,000 of the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to be manufactured by [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid Combat Automata]] after the [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]], led by [[Spar|Alpha-Ø2]], allied themselves with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The Battle Legionnaires were deployed successfully during the [[Battle of Kamino (Separatist–Mandalorian invasion)|Second Battle of Kamino]] and the [[Battle of New Bornalex]], but during the failed [[Mission to Norval II]], they suffered heavy losses at the hands of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] forces, along with their [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] masters. BL-17 was one of the few Battle Legionnaires to survive the Republic victory at the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]{{CSWECite|II|390|Norvall II}} [[Norval II]]. He was down but still functional, and was later found by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] during the early years of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Fett, who also had a Mandalorian heritage, recognized the [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid's]] potential and made BL-17 his aide. ===Mission to Boonta=== {{Quote|It's a pleasure to associate with a droid of comparable intellect…for a change.|C-3PO, on BL-17|}} In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], Fett was approached by the [[Crime lord/Legends|gangster]] [[Sise Fromm]] on{{DroidsCite|The Trigon Unleashed}} the planet [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]] to hunt the young [[Joben-Dusat Racing Team|racing team]] of [[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]] and [[Jord Dusat]], along with their [[Annoo resistance|rebel]] associate [[Kea Moll]], after the group destroyed Fromm's ''[[Trigon One]]'' weapons [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]], leaving the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]] powerless against their rivals. Though [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] had placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Fromm and his son [[Tig Fromm|Tig]], Fett agreed to take the job in return for a previous favor provided by the Fromm Gang. [[File:BL17AndC3PO-ARTTF.png|thumb|right|250px|BL-17 and C-3PO]] Fett's plan was to work against the group from within, so he dispatched BL-17 to locate and befriend the group's [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]. BL-17 soon achieved his goal by pretending to have graduated from the same production facility as the protocol droid. When C-3PO mentioned that Joben and Dusat needed somewhere to conduct repairs on their racing [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]], the ''[[White Witch]]'', ahead of the [[Boonta Speeder Race]], BL-17 offered him the use of a garage supposedly owned by his master, who, he explained, was away on business. C-3PO relayed the message to Joben, who gladly accepted and moved the [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] to the garage. BL-17 soon began using his friendship with C-3PO to keep the other droid away from his masters, leaving them vulnerable. Though he suggested that C-3PO's counterpart, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], might like to join the two droids, C-3PO resisted, stating that R2-D2 was not one for conversation. BL-17 soon saw an opportunity to make his move when Moll was the only [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] present in the garage. As he and C-3PO left the garage together, BL-17 smashed the door controls to seal Moll inside and remotely triggered a gas leak in the welding tanks to kill the [[Human/Legends|Human]]. However, BL-17's failure to persuade C-3PO to let R2-D2 accompany them caused the plan to fail when the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], unaffected by the gas, used a heavy lifter to smash open the door and pulled Moll clear of the building. ===Destruction=== {{Quote|I thought you were deactivated ages ago, old-timer. But I see you've saved that privilege for me.''"
"''Really now, BL-17, I don't care for this rustic any more than you, but I really don't think…''"
"''Put a clamp on it, chrome dome. I've been waiting for this moment.|BL-17 turns on C-3PO and Proto One|}} By the time BL-17 and C-3PO returned to the garage, Joben had taken Moll for medical treatment and only R2-D2 remained, keeping watch over the ''White Witch''. BL-17 suggested that the speeder was not safe in the now-doorless garage and that he knew of somewhere they could store it. C-3PO agreed and helped BL-17 move the ''White Witch'' to an isolated processing plant; blaming R2-D2 for the apparent accident with the welding tank, C-3PO refused to let the astromech droid go with them. [[File:ProtoOneGetsChucked-ARTTF.png|thumb|left|250px|BL-17 fights Proto One]] BL-17 took advantage of the situation by sending a signal to R2-D2, supposedly from C-3PO, to tell the other droid where to find them. The Battle Legionnaire knew that R2-D2 would lead his masters to the processing plant, where Boba Fett would be waiting for them. With C-3PO no longer needed, BL-17 prepared to eliminate the droid. However, he was interrupted by the arrival of [[Proto One]], an aging droid who disliked BL-17 from a prior encounter. Seeing the opportunity to eliminate two of his enemies at once, BL-17 produced his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and pointed it at Proto One. Still oblivious as to BL-17's true purpose, C-3PO tried to persuade him to stop, providing enough of a distraction for Proto One to charge at the battle droid. However, BL-17 acted quickly, grabbing the oncoming droid and throwing him aside before turning his attention back to C-3PO. Only the arrival of R2-D2, along with Joben and Moll, saved the protocol droid—as BL-17 turned his weapon to the new threats, C-3PO charged him, knocking the battle droid into a nearby pile of scrap machinery. BL-17 quickly righted himself, but the unstable junk heap collapsed, crushing the droid. Joben was able to escape the trap in the ''White Witch'', leading Fett away from the processing plant and Moll. Fett pursued his target even as he participated in the Boonta Speeder Race but, when the bounty hunter's ''[[Silver Speeder]]'' was destroyed, he decided to instead capture the Fromms and their assistant, [[Vlix Oncard]], turning the group over to Jabba the Hutt and using the reward to cover the loss of BL-17 and the ''Silver Speeder''. ==Characteristics== [[File:BL17Crushed-ARTTF.png|thumb|right|250px|BL-17, crushed by a pile of machinery]] Like all BL-series Battle Legionnaires, BL-17 was designed for combat. He had an internal first-aid kit, an inbuilt signal jammer, [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] that could double as spotlights and military-grade balance gyros that, when coupled with joints calibrated for maximum torque, allowed him to lift thousands of [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] and run at thirty [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers per hour]] for extended periods without recharging. He was armed with a [[Briletto Company|Briletto]] [[AAP-II blaster box|AAP-II "blaster box"]], which could be attached to his chest, and was finished with an olive-drab military camouflage to match the [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian Protectors' armor]]. His appearance was similar to the [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series protocol droid]], a similarity that aided him in befriending C-3PO. The droid was a cold and efficient killer—traits that were recognized by Boba Fett—and even enjoyed killing, expressing his eagerness to destroy both C-3PO and Proto One. BL-17 could be deceptive, a skill that assisted in his work with Fett, and proved successful at manipulating C-3PO and turning the apparent animosity between C-3PO and R2-D2 to his advantage. Boba Fett wrote in ''[[The Bounty Hunter Code]]'' that BL-17 was competent and versatile, and he planned to get a new droid after completing a job in the [[Mechis system/Legends|Mechis system]].''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]'' ==Behind the scenes== [[File:BL17-Credits.jpg|thumb|left|250px|BL-17 in the ''Star Wars: Droids'' opening credits]] BL-17 was created by [[Peter Sauder]] and [[Steven Wright]] for the animated series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']], making his first appearance in the final episode of the first story arc, "[[A Race to the Finish]]," first broadcast on [[September 28]], [[1985]].{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} The ''Star Wars: Droids'' opening credits depicted a scene in which BL-17, alongside another droid and Boba Fett, rode a [[Skiff/Legends|skiff]] through a desert canyon in search of R2-D2 and C-3PO. This scene is not taken from any episode of the series.''[[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]'' BL-17's involvement in the events of "A Race to the Finish" has since been referenced in several sources, though there are some discrepancies between these sources and the episode. The [[1994]] second edition of [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded|''A Guide to the Star Wars Universe'']] by [[Bill Slavicsek]] says that it is unknown whether Fett was still in possession of the droid following the episode.''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' This contradicts Fett's desire to cover the cost of BL-17's loss as depicted in "A Race to the Finish" and [[2006]]'s ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]], which confirm his destruction. [[1995]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' by [[Andy Mangels]] mentions the character in Boba Fett's entry, though it erroneously refers to him as "BR-17".''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' The [[June]] [[2004]] [[Polyhedron 170|''Polyhedron'' 170]] article ''[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]'' by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Rich Handley]] also recaps the episode, stating that BL-17 planted a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] in the ''White Witch'' while it was in the processing center.{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} In the episode, however, it is Tig Fromm and Vlix Oncard who plant the detonator. ==Appearances==
